title: A Psalm of David, when he fled from his son Absalom.

verses.1: O LORD, how my foes have increased! How many rise up against me!
verses.2: Many say of me, “God will not deliver him.” Selah
verses.3: But You, O LORD, are a shield around me, my glory, and the One who lifts my head.
verses.4: To the LORD I cry aloud, and He answers me from His holy mountain. Selah
verses.5: I lie down and sleep; I wake again, for the LORD sustains me.
verses.6: I will not fear the myriads set against me on every side.
verses.7: Arise, O LORD! Save me, O my God! Strike all my enemies on the jaw; break the teeth of the wicked.
verses.8: Salvation belongs to the LORD; may Your blessing be on Your people. Selah

metadata.source: Derived from the Berean Standard Bible (BSB). Missing verses restored from the Textus Receptus tradition for completeness.
